citations.description: All resolution tests use REBAR_TRACKER_DIR override; no fresh-linked-worktree regression test.
Gate implementation: ensure_store_mounted_best_effort() called before intercepts.

findings.criterion: rebar verify-commit-ticket in a fresh linked worktree (no .tickets-tracker) auto-mounts (symlink to main repo store) and resolves the id, instead of 'ticket store not found' — regression test.
findings.detail: The gate implementation exists in src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py (lines 406-407) and src/rebar/_cli/_init.py (ensure_store_mounted_best_effort), but the acceptance criterion explicitly requires a regression test for this scenario. No such test exists: a comprehensive search of all test files found no test that simulates a fresh linked worktree (no .tickets-tracker) and verifies that rebar verify-commit-ticket auto-mounts before resolving the id. The existing test_verify_commit_ticket.py tests all use REBAR_TRACKER_DIR override, which bypasses the gate entirely and does not exercise the worktree-symlink code path.

citations.description: _NO_AUTO_MOUNT only contains init and scratch; explain is not listed but is silently no-op through the gate.
Existing explain tests do not verify the mount-gate does not force-mount in a no-store context.

findings.criterion: explain (and init/scratch) are NOT force-mounted — they keep working with no store — guard test.
findings.detail: The code correctly excludes init and scratch from the gate via _NO_AUTO_MOUNT (line 42 of __init__.py), and explain works without a store because the gate is best-effort and silently no-ops when no attachable store exists. However, the acceptance criterion explicitly requires a guard test that verifies this behavior. No such test exists anywhere in the test suite: there is no test that verifies rebar explain works in a no-store, outside-repo, or fresh-worktree context specifically due to the gate not force-mounting. The existing explain tests (test_plan_review_explain.py) do not test the mount-gate exemption behavior.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
